name: Yellow wax bean (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Yellow wax bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) [Pulses] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: MANUAL, PHENOL EXPLORER, SELFNutritionData, USDA.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169320 (Beans, snap, yellow, raw)
VandenLangenberg, Bethke & Nienhuis 2012, Crop Science 52:1692, doi:10.2135/cropsci2011.07.0396 (fructose/glucose/sucrose in snap-bean pods)
VandenLangenberg et al. 2012, HortScience 47:874 (sugar accumulation patterns in snap/dry bean pods)
Sanchez-Mata et al. 2002, Eur Food Res Technol, doi:10.1007/s00217-001-0447-0 (soluble sugars in green beans by HPLC)
Lopez-Hernandez et al. 1996, Eur Food Res Technol, doi:10.1007/s002170050091 (organic acids of green beans: oxalic/malic/citric/fumaric; quinic absent)
Gonzalez-Castro et al. 1994, J AOAC Int 77:1056, PMID 8069112 (HPLC organic acids + vitamin C in green beans)
provenance.curation_notes: Yellow wax bean = yellow-podded cultivar of common snap bean (Phaseolus vulgaris); full macro/amino-acid/vitamin/mineral panel is high-confidence from USDA FDC 169320 (Beans, snap, yellow, raw). Dominant fermentable carbon sources are the monosaccharides fructose and glucose (fructose >= glucose > sucrose); it is a low-acid vegetable whose predominant organic acid is malic (then citric, oxalic; fumaric trace) - these organic acids were the main FooDB omission and are added here from Lopez-Hernan
curation_added: L-malic acid (predominant organic acid, presence + rank sourced; absolute value paywalled)
individual free sugars with amounts: D-fructose, D-glucose, sucrose
quantitative full mineral panel (Ca, Fe, Mg, P, K, Na, Zn, Cu, Mn, Se)

curation_removed.name: Creatine cytosol
curation_removed.reason: Animal muscle metabolite; not synthesized by Phaseolus; FooDB predicted/implausible for a plant food

curation_removed.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
curation_removed.reason: Animal-derived amino sulfonic acid; essentially absent in plants; FooDB predicted

curation_removed.name: Retinol
curation_removed.reason: Preformed (animal) vitamin A; USDA vitamin A RAE = 0 for yellow wax bean; plants supply provitamin-A carotenoids, not retinol; FooDB artifact

curation_removed.name: Racemethionine
curation_removed.reason: Synthetic DL-methionine; not a natural food constituent (natural form is L-methionine, already listed); FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: L-Methionine Sulfoxide
curation_removed.reason: Oxidation artifact of methionine, not a genuine nutrient of the fresh pod

curation_removed.name: NG,NG-Dimethyl-L-arginine
curation_removed.reason: ADMA is a mammalian NOS-pathway metabolite; not a plant growth nutrient; FooDB predicted

curation_removed.name: Histamine
curation_removed.reason: Trace biogenic amine, predicted; not a growth-relevant nutrient of fresh bean pods

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence_source: VandenLangenberg et al. 2012 Crop Science doi:10.2135/cropsci2011.07.0396 (range 0.25-1.78 g/100g FW); USDA FDC 169320 total sugars 3.27

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence_source: VandenLangenberg et al. 2012 doi:10.2135/cropsci2011.07.0396 (range 0.23-1.38 g/100g FW)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence_source: VandenLangenberg et al. 2012 doi:10.2135/cropsci2011.07.0396 (range 0.10-0.78 g/100g FW); FooDB FOOD00882 lists sucrose

components.name: myo-inositol
components.evidence_source: Sanchez-Mata et al. 2002 Eur Food Res Technol doi:10.1007/s00217-001-0447-0 (soluble sugars in green beans by HPLC)

components.name: L-malic acid
components.evidence_source: Lopez-Hernandez et al. 1996 Eur Food Res Technol doi:10.1007/s002170050091; Gonzalez-Castro/AOAC method 1994 (J AOAC Int 77:1056, PMID 8069112)

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence_source: Lopez-Hernandez et al. 1996 doi:10.1007/s002170050091; AOAC 1994 PMID 8069112

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence_source: Lopez-Hernandez et al. 1996 doi:10.1007/s002170050091; AOAC 1994 PMID 8069112

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence_source: Lopez-Hernandez et al. 1996 doi:10.1007/s002170050091; AOAC 1994 PMID 8069112

components.name: L-DOPA / dopamine (trace catecholamines; low confidence for Phaseolus pods)
